headline: The GOP-led House voted to restrict President Trump’s ability to continue the war against Iran without congressional approval, the latest sign that Republican lawmakers’ support for the war is eroding
Months after the Supreme Court struck down President Trump’s most sweeping global tariffs, the administration said it would levy new tariffs using a different legal mechanism
CBS News boss Bari Weiss sounds off on Scott Pelley firing in dramatic all-hands staff call: ‘Path that he chose’
California Democrats head to a primary election on Tuesday after a raucous race to help determine a successor to Gov. Gavin Newsom
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has blocked the promotion of eight Navy captains to be one-star admirals, including two female and two Black officers, according to current and former U.S. officials
Berkshire Hathaway’s $6.8 billion deal to acquire a major home builder reflects its conviction that the housing market will shake off its long slump and recover as it always has
The wife of Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner last year told the campaign about texts she had found between Platner and other women
Ukrainian drones hit St. Petersburg oil terminal before city hosts Russian economic forum

The case for raising the Bank of England’s key interest rate grows stronger the longer the conflict in the Middle East continues, and action may be needed within the next few weeks, a member of the Monetary Policy Committee said Tuesday
Israel strikes southern Lebanon but partial truce with Hezbollah appears to hold
